meta.per_capita_lpd_source: City of Ottawa 2023 Drinking Water Quality Report
data_licences: Open Government Licence – Canada (Environment and Climate Change Canada)

source.primary: Ottawa River (primary, ~90% via Britannia WTP) + Rideau River (secondary, ~10% via Lemieux Island WTP)
source.wfi_note: Ottawa and Rideau River watersheds have low wildfire interface risk

direct_intake.name: No reservoir — direct river intakes
direct_intake.data_note: Ottawa draws directly from the Ottawa and Rideau Rivers with no storage reservoir.

storage.data_note: No source storage — similar vulnerability to Edmonton but mitigated by dual-river sourcing and higher baseline flows.

plants.name: Britannia WTP
plants.process: conventional + UV + chlorination + fluoride. Draws from Ottawa River.

plants.name: Lemieux Island WTP
plants.process: conventional + UV + chlorination + fluoride. Draws from Rideau River.

treatment.data_note: Dual-source design provides operational redundancy. Rideau River has chronic cyanobacteria issues in late summer.

restriction.hypothesized_triggers: Ottawa uses seasonal watering bylaws (odd/even). Full restrictions historically rare.

risk.single_point_of_failure: Ottawa River is primary source for ~90% of supply. A contamination event upstream could force full reliance on Rideau River, which has capacity for only ~25% of demand.
risk.climate_note: Rideau River cyanobacteria blooms are increasing in frequency with warming summers.

use.data_note: Sector split approximate from City of Ottawa annual report

egress.cso_note: Combined sewers in older urban areas. ROPEC handles all Ottawa wastewater.
